summary.en: Built in the 19th century under Khedive Ismail to be a "Paris on the Nile," this modern city center is a chaotic, vibrant hub of commerce, revolution history, and bustling street life.

This district is the historic medieval heart of the city, dating back to the 10th century. It is an incredibly atmospheric area characterized by historic mosques, ancient architecture, Turkish baths, and bustling bazaars.

Located south of the center, Ma'adi is a quiet, leafy, and affluent residential district that caters to many foreign expatriates. It offers a much-needed escape from the congestion of historic Cairo with a focus on nature and relaxed dining.
Situated on the west bank of the Nile in Giza, these sprawling riverside districts are characterized by modern concrete, upscale shopping, and a vibrant cultural and nightlife scene.

Located south of downtown, this is the historical kernel of Cairo, containing the site of the original 641 CE Arab tent city of Al-Fustat. It is a deeply historical and peaceful district filled with ancient religious sites.

Located on a large island in the middle of the Nile, Zamalek is a quiet, upmarket district known for its embassies, leafy promenades, and high-end shopping. It offers a sophisticated, relaxed atmosphere away from the mainland's intense congestion.

description.en: The historic museum holding countless ancient Egyptian artifacts, located north of Tahrir Square.
A renowned establishment offering a highly authentic Egyptian gastronomical experience.
A small, independent theater known for hosting cultural plays and independent musicians.
The city's most famous spot for authentic koshari in the downtown area, though it has multiple outlets.
Local vendors selling traditional hibiscus tea, served warm in winter or cold in summer.
A massive, crowded street market selling inexpensive books, electronics, and clothing.
A bustling commercial square surrounded by local shoe shops, bookstores, and sweet vendors.
An independent contemporary art space that serves as a vital cultural gathering spot.

name.en: The Townhouse Gallery of Contemporary Art

description.en: A beautifully landscaped garden providing a stellar vantage point over the district.
Located on a narrow street near the souq, this venue hosts free traditional Sufi dancing performances.
A grand castle built by Salah Al-Din, featuring ancient water pipes that once carried Nile water to the fortress.
An iconic, historic coffee shop nestled in the Khan El-Khalili bazaar, perfect for people-watching.
Independent street-side stalls serving feTīr, a traditional Egyptian pie that can be made sweet with honey or savory with meat and cheese.
Cairo's main historic souq where merchants sell everything from spices to gold.
Local street carts serving Cairo's ultimate comfort food: a mix of rice, macaroni, lentils, chick peas, and tomato sauce.
Any small street-corner coffee house (maqhâ) serving authentic sweet Turkish coffee ('ahwe turki).
A high-quality option located in Ma'adi, offering a stylish take on traditional Egyptian gastronomy.
A beloved local quality restaurant offering fantastic, authentic home-style Egyptian food.
A scenic river promenade perfect for a quiet afternoon stroll.
Local residential fruit stalls serving fakhfakhenna, an indulgent traditional Egyptian fruit salad/juice hybrid.
A local art gallery showcasing works by Egyptian artists, reflecting the neighborhood's cultural side.
Side streets of Ma'adi dedicated to selling superb local vegetables and fruits delivered by bicycle.
Street-side vendors pouring traditional sweet and sour tamarind juice.
A massive 60-square-kilometer desert park on the edge of Ma'adi, offering trekking, cycling, and rock climbing.
A well-regarded local establishment serving high-quality, authentic Egyptian food.
A fresh juice bar serving a variety of refreshing fruit drinks popular in the area.
A famous independent nightlife and music venue offering a relaxed atmosphere and drinks.
Opened in 1891, this is one of the oldest zoological gardens in the world.
A fairly large, historic botanical park near the zoo, filled with diverse trees and flowers.
A friendly cultural hub mixing locals and international students.
Independent vendors serving Lebanese/Syrian-style roasted meat wrapped in fresh bread on commercial streets.
A local coffee shop offering traditional Egyptian beverages and a relaxed atmosphere.
Small bakeries selling incredibly cheap, fresh-baked goods and date-stuffed treats.
An ancient religious site near the roots of the original "City of the Tents".
A sweeping, modern museum offering a massive overview of Egyptian culture through the ages.
Local, unnamed street vendors selling Cairo's traditional bean paste and falafel staples.
A historic island in the Nile connected to Old Cairo's history.
Spots selling sahleb, a comforting, thick milk-based winter beverage, ideal for colder months.
A historic enclave housing the famous "Hanging Church" (Church of the Virgin Mary).
Local street vendors serving traditional loose "dust tea" (kûshari) steeped with fresh mint leaves.
A 185-meter landmark offering unparalleled views of the city.
A charming, primarily English-language local bookstore.
A great independent shop selling high-quality crafts made by local artisans.
A unique park featuring large caves equipped with water aquariums.

name.en: Genenet El Asmak (Garden of the Fish)

description.en: A highly regarded independent restaurant specializing in excellent vegetarian food.
A beloved local staple (with a few branches) serving some of the best pizza in Cairo.
Local stalls pressing fresh, seasonal juices like mango (manga) or guava (gawafa).
An organic store offering local healthy goods and beverages.
One of the most famous falafel and ful (fava bean) carts in downtown Cairo, offering a variety of traditional Egyptian breakfast platters.
A cozy, traditional Egyptian café with decor unchanged since the 1960s, offering a panoramic view of the bustling Tahrir Square.
A recently renovated public space outside the magnificent Abdeen Palace, now featuring excellent food courts perfect for families.

name.en: Monastery of Saint Simon (The Cave Church)

description.en: An architectural wonder carved directly into the Mokattam mountain, this massive cave church is one of the largest Christian churches in the Middle East.
A vast and ancient necropolis where generations of Cairenes have lived and worked among historic tombs and mausoleums.
A historic street in the heart of Islamic Cairo, often called an open-air museum for its concentration of medieval Islamic architecture.
A pair of beautifully preserved 16th and 17th-century houses filled with an eclectic collection of art and artifacts, offering a glimpse into historic Cairene life.
A quiet and productive co-working space in Ma'adi, ideal for reading, writing, or attending workshops and cultural events.
A vintage Swiss restaurant offering a unique European dining experience in Cairo, particularly famous for its classic cheese fondue.
An ancient, intricate structure on Rhoda Island used for centuries to measure the clarity and water level of the Nile River during the annual flood.
A preserved oasis of tranquility with traditional Andalusian-style architecture, mosaics, and beautiful views of the Nile.
An arts house in a vintage building where you can enjoy a drink, live music, and cultural events in a setting that feels like a 1950s movie.
A quiet and cozy cafe and bookstore, perfect for working or relaxing while listening to Sufi chants and attending cultural events.

prompt.en: Photograph the grand exterior architecture of the museum.
Capture a photo of an interesting ancient Egyptian artifact inside the museum (if permitted).
Photograph a traditional Egyptian meat or vegetable dish from their menu.
Capture the unique decor and ambiance that makes Felfela an authentic experience.
Take a photo of the theater's marquee or a local playbill.
Capture a detail that highlights the independent and cultural spirit of the Housaper Theater.
Snap a picture of the bustling, fast-paced kitchen staff assembling orders.
Capture a close-up of a freshly prepared bowl of koshari, ready to be served.
Take a photo of the deep, rich crimson color of your karkadee tea.
Photograph the vendor preparing the karkadee, highlighting its refreshing nature.
Scavenge for the oldest-looking Arabic book you can find and take a picture of its cover.
Photograph a unique or unexpected item for sale amidst the books and electronics.
Safely photograph the "Frogger"-style street crossing techniques locals use to navigate the chaotic traffic.
Capture the lively atmosphere of Midan Talaat Harb, focusing on the surrounding shops and vendors.
Photograph the most striking piece of contemporary local art on display.
Capture the atmosphere of the gallery as a vital cultural gathering spot.
Photograph the historic Islamic Cairo skyline from the park's elevated viewpoints.
Capture a serene moment within the beautifully landscaped gardens of Al-Azhar Park.
Capture the dizzying blur of the Al Tanura Troupe mid-spin.
Photograph the ancient architecture of the Al-Ghouri Mausoleum itself.
Scavenge for the remains of the historic Majra Al-Oyouon water pipes and take a picture.
Photograph the imposing architecture and scale of the Citadel of Mohamed Ali Pasha.
Take a photo of the traditional "double apple" flavored shisha water pipe.
Capture a candid shot of people enjoying the atmosphere at this iconic coffee shop.
Take a video of the vendor expertly stretching and folding the feTīr dough.
Photograph your chosen feTīr, highlighting whether it's sweet or savory.
Haggle for a rustic glassware piece or perfume bottle and photograph your prize.
Capture the vibrant chaos and diverse goods on display within the Khan El Khalily Bazaar.
Capture a close-up photo of the colorful layers of a freshly assembled koshari bowl.
Photograph the lively atmosphere around a traditional koshari cart.
Photograph your tiny cup of 'ahwe turki alongside the bustling street scene.
Zoom in on a unique architectural detail or decoration of the traditional coffee house.
Take a picture of your lavishly presented traditional Egyptian meal.
Photograph an element of the restaurant's interior that reflects its stylish take on Egyptian cuisine.
Photograph the colorful, contemporary decor that the restaurant is known for.
Capture a photo of a delicious home-style Egyptian dish from Cairo Kitchen's menu.
Capture a serene sunset photo over the Nile river from the promenade.
Photograph a moment that conveys the peacefulness of a stroll along the Corniche el-Nil.
Take a colorful photo of your fakhfakhenna showcasing the different fruit layers.
Photograph the toppings or presentation that make fakhfakhenna an indulgent treat.
Photograph a piece of art that you find particularly representative of Egyptian culture.
Capture the inviting interior of the art gallery, showcasing its layout and atmosphere.
Find a market seller and take a vibrant photo of fresh, imperfectly shaped Roma tomatoes.
Capture a photo of produce being delivered by bicycle, a common sight in the souqs.
Photograph the vendor pouring the dark juice into your glass.
Capture the refreshing look of a glass of tamarind juice on a warm day.
Scavenge for and photograph local desert wildlife, such as local reptiles or deer.
Capture the expansive desert landscape of Wadi Digla, highlighting its natural beauty.
Capture a photo of a beautifully arranged traditional Middle Eastern meat platter.
Photograph the interior of Abou Shakra, highlighting its authentic Egyptian dining ambiance.
Order a fresh juice and take a vibrant photo of your drink.
Photograph the array of fresh fruits available at the juice bar.
Take a photo of the live local musicians performing on stage.
Capture the relaxed and vibrant nightlife atmosphere of the Cairo Jazz Club.
Photograph the historic 19th-century architecture and enclosures inside the zoo.
Capture a photo of one of the zoo's animal residents in its historic setting.
Scavenge for the most exotic flower you can find in the park and snap a close-up.
Capture a wide shot showcasing the diverse trees and plant life in Al Orman Gardens.
Photograph a cultural flyer or Arabic language poster on their bulletin boards.
Capture a moment showing the mix of locals and international students at the center.
Take an action shot of the vendor carving the roasted meat off the vertical spit.
Photograph a freshly prepared shawarma sandwich, ready to be enjoyed.
Photograph a traditional coffee or tea being served in a local Mohandeseen coffee shop.
Capture the relaxed ambiance of a typical neighborhood coffee shop in Mohandeseen.
Scavenge for an Italian-style breadstick covered in nigella and sesame seeds and photograph it.
Find and photograph a date-stuffed treat, highlighting its sweet appeal.
Photograph the serene, expansive courtyard of the mosque.
Find and photograph an ancient architectural detail within the mosque.
Capture a photo of your favorite historical artifact on display.
Photograph the modern architecture of the National Museum of Egyptian Civilization.
Take a photo of your fresh, locally-made falāfel sandwich.
Capture the simplicity and authenticity of a fūl or falāfel stall.
Take a landscape photo of the Nile from the island's banks.
Capture a photo that conveys the historic and peaceful atmosphere of Rhoda Island.
Take a cozy photo of your warm sahleb drink against the backdrop of ancient ruins.
Photograph the preparation of sahleb, emphasizing its comforting nature in winter.
Photograph the unique architectural elevation that gives the "Hanging Church" its name.
Capture the peaceful and historic atmosphere within the Coptic Quarter.
Photograph the floating fresh mint leaves inside your traditional tea glass.
Capture a photo that illustrates the traditional way mint tea is served or enjoyed.
Use the 360-degree view to capture the distant Giza Pyramids on the western horizon.
Photograph the winding Nile River as it flows through the heart of Cairo from the tower's height.
Find a book about Egyptian history and photograph its cover.
Capture a photo that conveys the charming and inviting atmosphere of this independent bookstore.
Find a piece of jewelry or craft and photograph its intricate handmade details.
Find an item and imagine the artisan who made it. Photograph the item in a way that tells its story.
Take a photo of the underwater life through the glass windows of the park's caves.
Capture the lush greenery and peaceful atmosphere of the Garden of the Fish.
Photograph a meat-free dish that highlights local Egyptian vegetable flavors.
Take a photo that captures the sophisticated and relaxed ambiance of L'aubergine.
Scavenge for the most uniquely Egyptian pizza topping on the menu and snap a picture of your slice.
Capture a close-up photo of the perfectly baked crust of a Maison Thomas pizza.
Order a drink in Arabic and take a photo of the vibrant, freshly pressed juice.
Capture a photo of your juice with the bustling street life in the background.
Find and photograph the most unique organic Egyptian ingredient on the shelves.
Purchase a local healthy snack and photograph its packaging or presentation.
Find a building with classic European-style architecture and capture a detail that looks like it's from a century ago.
Take a selfie in the middle of a bustling 'midan' (square), capturing the energy of the city behind you.
Find a street vendor selling a classic Egyptian snack and take an artistic photo of the food they're preparing.
Search the side streets for a faded, hand-painted, or neon sign from a bygone era and snap a picture of it.
Photograph the most interesting book cover you can find at one of the many sidewalk bookseller stalls.
Capture the 'organized chaos' of Downtown traffic. Try a low angle to make the cars and buses look monumental.
Take a photo of a classic 'ahwa' (coffee shop) scene, focusing on the people, the shisha pipes, or the games being played.
Find a reflection of a historic Downtown building in a modern glass window or a puddle.
Look up! Photograph the most interesting or ornate balcony you can find.
Find a grand, old doorway and take a photo that makes you wonder what's behind it.
Grab a local pastry or sweet from a bakery and take a happy selfie with your treat.
Capture an interesting pattern of shadows cast by the tall buildings onto the street or sidewalk.
Discreetly capture a candid moment of daily life: someone reading a paper, hailing a cab, or chatting with a friend.
Find a vibrant splash of color against the neutral tones of the city buildings—a colorful scarf, a bright sign, or a painted shutter.
Get up close and personal with the city. Take a photo of the texture of a weathered wall, a metal grate, or a tiled entryway.
Capture the silhouette of a mosque's minaret against the sky. Bonus points for a sunset or sunrise shot!
Find a narrow, winding alleyway and photograph the way the light filters down between the buildings.
Spot a local craftsman—a metalworker, a carpenter, a weaver—and respectfully capture a photo of their hands at work.
Find the most colorful display in the bazaar, whether it's a pile of spices, a rack of lanterns, or a collection of textiles.
Take a selfie in front of a massive, ancient, and ornate doorway. Try to look as epic as the door!
Get close to a wall, gate, or window and take a photo focusing on the intricate geometric patterns of Islamic art.
The cats are the true locals here. Find a photogenic feline lounging in a historic setting and snap its portrait.
Capture a classic scene at a street 'ahwa' (cafe): a glass of mint tea, a backgammon game, or friends in conversation.
Climb to a high vantage point (like the top of a minaret or a rooftop) and capture the sprawling view of the historic district.
Find a local bakery ('furn') and photograph the fresh 'aish baladi' (flatbread) being made or stacked high.
Look for a building with a 'mashrabiya' (projecting oriel window with latticework) and capture its intricate details.
Find a 'sabil' (a public water fountain) and photograph its unique architecture, a testament to historic public works.
Try to capture the feeling of the souq's sounds in a single photo. Focus on a haggling merchant, a shouting vendor, or a curious customer.
Find an interesting mosque dome and take a picture that highlights its shape, texture, or the way the light hits it.
Capture the steam rising from a cart selling roasted sweet potatoes, corn, or a hot koshari.
Find the most beautiful tree-lined street in Ma'adi and take a photo looking down the road, creating a tunnel of green.
Photograph the architectural details of one of Ma'adi's charming old villas—a unique gate, a tiled porch, or a blooming garden.
Head to the Corniche and take a selfie with a traditional felucca sailing boat on the Nile in the background.
Find a cozy-looking outdoor cafe and capture a quiet moment: a person reading, a cup of coffee, or an empty chair waiting for a friend.
Visit a local fruit and vegetable stand and create an artistic shot of the colorful, fresh produce.
Keep your eyes peeled for hidden street art or graffiti and snap a picture of your favorite piece.
Ma'adi is known for its gardens. Find the most vibrant bougainvillea, jasmine, or other flowering plant and take a close-up photo.
Spot a classic or vintage car parked on one of Ma'adi's quiet streets and take a cool photo of it.
Find a friendly-looking neighborhood cat or dog and (from a respectful distance) take its portrait.
Capture the colors of the sunset over the Nile from the Ma'adi Corniche.
Grab a fresh juice from a local stall and take a refreshing selfie with your colorful drink.
Photograph the interesting patterns created by the shadows of leaves and branches on a sidewalk or wall.
Find a small, local grocery shop ('ba'al') and capture its character—the stacked goods, the owner, or the hand-written signs.
Focus on the beautiful and intricate designs of a wrought iron gate or fence, common on Ma'adi's older properties.
Find a quiet bench or spot and take a photo that captures the peaceful, suburban feeling of Ma'adi.
As dusk falls, capture the vibrant glow of a neon sign from a shop or restaurant.
This area is fashionable. Find someone with great street style and (respectfully) ask to take a photo of an interesting detail of their outfit.
Find a bustling shawarma stand and take an action shot of the chef expertly carving the meat.
Treat yourself to an elaborate dessert from one of the many cafes or patisseries and take a delighted selfie with it.
Look up and capture the towering height and modern architecture of a residential or commercial building.
Photograph the colorful array of fresh fruits on display at a local juice bar.
Find a small, quiet park or a tree-lined median and take a photo that contrasts the green space with the surrounding urban hustle.
Find the most creative or luxurious window display in one of the area's many boutiques and snap a picture.
Capture the ambiance of a modern coffee shop—the latte art, the focused remote workers, or the stylish decor.
From Agouza or Dokki, find a spot with a view of the Nile and capture the river scene with the city in the background.
From a safe spot like a pedestrian bridge, take a long-exposure shot of the car lights creating streaks of color at night.
Hunt for a building with distinctive 1960s or 70s architecture and photograph its unique design elements.
Capture a candid moment of daily life on a side street, away from the main commercial avenues.
Find a local bakery and take a mouth-watering photo of the rows of bread, croissants, or other baked goods.
Find a modern glass building and capture the reflection of the bustling street scene in its facade.
Find a wall made of ancient stone and take a close-up shot that highlights its texture and history.
Step inside a church, mosque, or synagogue and capture the way light streams through a window, creating a peaceful or dramatic effect.
Find a view where you can see symbols of different faiths (e.g., a cross and a crescent) in the same frame.
Take a selfie on a bridge crossing the Nile, with the water and Rhoda Island in the background.
In the Fustat area, find a pottery workshop or shop and photograph the beautiful patterns and shapes of the ceramics.
Take a low-angle photo of a cobblestone or stone-paved alleyway, making it look like a path into history.
Capture the silhouette of a Coptic church's bell tower against the bright Cairo sky.
Find a neighborhood stall selling fūl and falāfel and take a delicious-looking photo of this essential Egyptian meal.
Find an example of old, intricate wooden latticework on a window or balcony and capture its detailed craftsmanship.
Capture a scene that feels timeless and peaceful within the ancient walls of Old Cairo.
Photograph a piece of Coptic iconography or art, focusing on the unique style and colors.
Use an ancient stone archway to frame a view of a courtyard, a street, or another building.
Find a spot overlooking the Nile and capture a scene of life on the river—a fishing boat, a ferry, or birds on the water.
Peek through a gate or doorway to find and photograph a hidden, quiet courtyard.
Find a set of old, worn stone steps and take a photo that makes you think of the millions of footsteps that have passed over them.
Zamalek is full of beautiful apartment buildings. Find the most elegant or plant-filled balcony and capture its charm.
Walk along the edge of the island and capture the reflection of a bridge, a building, or the city lights in the waters of the Nile.
Find a quirky or artistic item in the window of one of Zamalek's many independent boutiques and take a photo of it.
Find a lush, green garden or park space and take a selfie surrounded by nature in the heart of the city.
Hunt for a building with classic Art Deco architecture and photograph a specific detail, like a curved corner, a geometric pattern, or a stylish doorway.
Find a piece of public art or a sculpture in a square or garden and take a creative photo with it.
Capture the peaceful atmosphere of a quiet, tree-lined residential street in Zamalek.
Take a photo that captures the vibe of a chic Zamalek café—the stylish patrons, the artistic decor, or the perfect cup of coffee.
Photograph the impressive entrance of one of Zamalek's old apartment buildings or embassies.
Find a spot that gives you a great view of the Cairo Tower from within the neighborhood and frame it in an interesting way.
Find a local fruit juice stall and take a colorful photo of the fresh juices or the fruit displays.
Take a photo of one of the bridges connecting Zamalek to the mainland, focusing on its structure and the flow of traffic.
Find a bookstore and take a photo of an interesting display or a quiet reading nook.
The cats of Zamalek are famously chic. Find one striking a pose in a garden or on a windowsill and take its portrait.
From the western side of the island, capture the sun setting over the Nile and the Agouza/Mohandeseen skyline.
